segments.text:
My soul is weary of my life. I will give free course to my complaint. 
I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I will tell God, ‘Do not condemn me. Show me why you contend with me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
Is it good to you that you should oppress, that you should despise the work of your hands, 
and smile on the counsel of the wicked?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Do you have eyes of flesh? Or do you see as man sees?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Are your days as the days of mortals, or your years as man’s years,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: that you inquire after my iniquity, and search after my sin?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Although you know that I am not wicked, there is no one who can deliver out of your hand.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “‘Your hands have framed me and fashioned me altogether, yet you destroy me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Remember, I beg you, that you have fashioned me as clay. Will you bring me into dust again?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Haven’t you poured me out like milk, and curdled me like cheese?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You have clothed me with skin and flesh, and knit me together with bones and sinews.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You have granted me life and loving kindness. Your visitation has preserved my spirit.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Yet you hid these things in your heart. I know that this is with you:
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: if I sin, then you mark me. You will not acquit me from my iniquity.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
If I am wicked, woe to me. If I am righteous, I still will not lift up my head, 
being filled with disgrace, 
and conscious of my affliction.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If my head is held high, you hunt me like a lion. Again you show yourself powerful to me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
You renew your witnesses against me, and increase your indignation on me. 
Changes and warfare are with me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: “‘Why, then, have you brought me out of the womb? I wish I had given up the spirit, and no eye had seen me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I should have been as though I had not been. I should have been carried from the womb to the grave.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Aren’t my days few? Stop! Leave me alone, that I may find a little comfort,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
before I go where I will not return from, 
to the land of darkness and of the shadow of death;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
the land dark as midnight, of the shadow of death, 
without any order, 
where the light is as midnight.’
segments.speaker: Unknown
